title: Rental Deposit Assistance * Families with Children - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rental Deposit Assistance * Families with Children 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers AIDS/HIV Families with Children Foster Children Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Medicaid Recipients Native American Community * Homelessness Issues Older Adults Pregnant Individuals Slavic Community Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Familes with children under age 18 who are currently housed and an income level at or below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) with an imminent risk of homelessness. Priority is given to SUN school families who identify as Asian Pacific Islanders or African Immigrants. Hours: No standard hours (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Familes with children under age 18 who are currently housed and an income level at or below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) with an imminent risk of homelessness. Priority is given to SUN school families who identify as Asian Pacific Islanders or African Immigrants.
